reason: Response A's sustained, surprising metaphors (the parabola/river of an academic arc, "old wood takes stain"), its signature reframing moves ("This is not a disadvantage dressed up in consolation"), the self-aware tricolon "living, enrolled, slightly nervous evidence," and the short punchy closer after dense prose all match my idea-forward, image-rich register on an open creative prompt. Response B is warm and competent but leans on conventional motivational beats ("the time was going to pass either way," "I'm rooting for you"), reading more like an imitation reaching for greeting-card sincerity. The conceptual density and aphoristic turns in A are the stronger fingerprint of my own writing.
